quests.description: Ask the Nuno sa Punso how to make amends.
talkDialogueLines: Hmph! You dare tread upon my mound, mortal?
This land remembers what your kind has done. The roots cry out, the soil weeps.
But... I sense something different in you. The mark of the Albularyo.
Perhaps you are not beyond redemption. Perhaps the spirits chose you for a reason.
If you wish to make amends, you must prove your respect for the earth.
Bring me healing herbs from the wild — Aloe, Geranium, Sugar Cane, and Fiber Leaves.
Only then can we perform the cleansing ritual and restore what was broken.
Now go, Albularyo. The night in this island is not friendly.

quests.description: Gather herbs in the wild for the Nuno's ritual.

objectives.description: Gather 3 Aloes, 3 Geranium, 3 Sugar Cane, and 5 Fiber Leaves.

quests.description: Bring the herbs to the Nuno sa Punso to complete the ritual.
talkDialogueLines: You have returned... and with the herbs I asked for. Good.
Aloe for healing, Geranium for protection, Sugar Cane for sweetness of spirit, and Fiber Leaves to bind it all together.
The old ways still hold power, Albularyo. Remember that.
With these, the ritual is complete. The mound is cleansed, and the spirits are at peace... for now.
You have earned my trust. From this day, you may trade with me for supplies.
But your journey is far from over. There is a broken ship along the coast.
Find it. Gather what remains of its hull. That vessel is your path forward.
Be warned — the creatures that roam this island will not let you pass easily.
Go now. The sea waits for no one.

quests.description: Search for the shipwreck in the coastal area.
Gather ship remnants from the wreckage.

objectives.description: Collect 3 ship remnants from the wreckage.

quests.description: Go to the broken ship to begin repairs.
The Kapre has appeared. Defeat it to continue.
Return to the ship and complete the repairs.
